name: CVC
description: Control Voltage Converter for EaganMatrix instruments. A single unit produces 16 high-resolution CV outputs (four per voice, four voices) via 16-bit DACs on -9.9V to +10V 3.5 mm jacks, fed by i2c from a Continuum, ContinuuMini, EaganMatrix Module/Micro or Osmose.

inputs.name: i2c IN (3.5 mm TRS)
inputs.description: Primary i2c input for Slim Continuum, ContinuuMini, EaganMatrix Module and EaganMatrix Micro.

inputs.name: i2c IN (DIN5)
inputs.description: Alternate i2c connection carried over unused pins of a MIDI cable, for older Thick Continuums and the Expressive E Osmose.

outputs.name: W1-4
outputs.description: Four configurable CV outputs normally assigned to gate per voice. 16-bit DAC.

outputs.name: X1-4
outputs.description: Four configurable CV outputs normally assigned to pitch (V/oct) per voice. 16-bit DAC, ~8-octave bend range preserved.

outputs.name: Y1-4
outputs.description: Four configurable CV outputs normally assigned to timbre per voice. 16-bit DAC.

outputs.name: Z1-4
outputs.description: Four configurable CV outputs normally assigned to pressure per voice. 16-bit DAC.

outputs.name: MIDI THRU (3.5 mm TRS Type A)
outputs.description: TRS Type A MIDI Thru that passes along MIDI received at the i2c DIN5 input.

outputs.name: VOICE ACTIVITY LEDs
outputs.description: One LED per voice; flashes when performance data is present on that voice.

behaviors.name: Four-voice mapping
behaviors.trigger: single CVC attached to an EaganMatrix source
behaviors.description: One unit maps the performance stream to four voices, each exposing four CVs (W/X/Y/Z) — 16 outputs total.

behaviors.name: Dual CVC expansion
behaviors.description: A paired setup expands to eight voices and 32 CVs using the same i2c protocol, no extra config in the module.

behaviors.name: Dynamic Base Voice
behaviors.trigger: subset of CVC outputs enabled in the EaganMatrix editor
behaviors.description: Only selected outputs are active, letting one performance switch between several monophonic patches on the fly.

behaviors.name: 16-bit expressive path
behaviors.description: 16-bit DACs and i2c transport preserve percussive attacks and wide pitch bends that standard MIDI-CV converters smooth away.

behaviors.name: MIDI Thru passthrough
behaviors.trigger: MIDI fed into the i2c DIN5 input
behaviors.description: MIDI arriving on the DIN5 i2c jack is echoed out the TRS Type A MIDI Thru for chaining to other gear.

behaviors.name: Voice activity LEDs
behaviors.description: Each voice LED flashes with its voice's activity, making routing visible at a glance.

patchIdeas.name: Four-voice translation
patchIdeas.patch: Connect a Continuum or EaganMatrix source to the CVC · route W for gate, X for pitch (V/oct), Y for timbre and Z for pressure · patch the four outputs of voice 1 to a single analog voice to hear one finger cleanly.
steps: In the EaganMatrix editor set Voices = 4 and enable standard W/X/Y/Z assignments
Patch the source's i2c out to the CVC i2c IN (3.5 mm TRS)
Patch X1 to your VCO V/oct, W1 to an envelope or VCA gate
Patch Y1 to filter cutoff, Z1 to VCA CV for dynamics
Play one finger on the Continuum and confirm only voice 1's LED flashes
patchIdeas.signalOut: One expressive monophonic voice that tracks pitch, timbre and pressure with Continuum-grade resolution.
patchIdeas.hear: A single clear note that follows finger position and pressure; attacks are percussive, not smoothed like a MIDI-CV converter.
patchIdeas.diagram:
CVC:
* Voices: 4
* W=Gate X=Pitch Y=Timbre Z=Pressure

VCO:
Filter:
VCA:

- Continuum (i2c) >> CVC (i2c IN 3.5mm)
- CVC (X1) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- CVC (W1) g> VCA (Gate)
- CVC (Y1) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- CVC (Z1) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In) -> VCA (In) -> Output

patchIdeas.name: Pressure-fanned filter voice
patchIdeas.patch: Use voice 1 only: X1 drives pitch, Y1 opens the filter for timbre, and Z1 fans out to both VCA depth and an extra filter resonance CV so harder pressing blooms the voice.
steps: Assign Voices = 1 (or just use voice 1 outputs) in the editor
Patch X1 to VCO V/oct and W1 to the envelope trigger
Patch Y1 to filter cutoff CV input A
Multiple Z1 to VCA CV and filter resonance CV
Play softly then press harder to hear the bloom
patchIdeas.signalOut: Monophonic voice whose brightness and dynamics scale with finger pressure.
patchIdeas.hear: Quiet attacks stay dark and thin; leaning into the Continuum opens the filter and swells the VCA simultaneously.
patchIdeas.diagram:
CVC:
* Voices: 1 active

VCO:
Filter:
VCA:
Mult:

- Continuum (i2c) >> CVC (i2c IN 3.5mm)
- CVC (X1) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- CVC (W1) g> Envelope (Trig)
- CVC (Y1) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- CVC (Z1) -> Mult (In)
- Mult (Out1) >> VCA (CV)
- Mult (Out2) >> Filter (Res CV)
- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In) -> VCA (In) -> Output

patchIdeas.name: Eight-voice stack with dual CVCs
patchIdeas.patch: Chain two CVCs over i2c · CVC A carries voices 1-4 for a polyphonic pad, CVC B carries voices 5-8 for a bass split · same EaganMatrix performance fans out across the rack for 32 CVs total.
steps: In the editor set Voices = 8 and split the keyboard: voices 1-4 high, 5-8 low
Patch the Continuum i2c to CVC A (3.5 mm TRS)
Daisy-chain CVC A to CVC B via i2c
Patch X1-4/W1-4 from CVC A to four pad VCOs and gates
Patch X1-4/W1-4 from CVC B to bass VCOs on a second voice card
patchIdeas.signalOut: Eight simultaneously expressive voices: four pad, four bass, all from one Continuum.
patchIdeas.hear: Chords in the upper half of the Continuum bloom as an expressive pad while low-hand notes trigger a separate bass engine.
patchIdeas.diagram:
CVC A:
CVC B:
* Voices: 8 (split)

Pad VCO 1-4:
Bass VCO 1-4:

- Continuum (i2c) >> CVC A (i2c IN)
- CVC A (i2c) >> CVC B (i2c IN)
- CVC A (X1..X4) p> Pad VCO 1..4 (V/Oct)
- CVC A (W1..W4) g> Pad Env 1..4 (Trig)
- CVC B (X1..X4) p> Bass VCO 1..4 (V/Oct)
- CVC B (W1..W4) g> Bass Env 1..4 (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Dynamic Base Voice patch-switcher
patchIdeas.patch: Configure two output subsets in the editor · subset A = voice 1 outputs to lead VCO, subset B = voice 2 outputs to bass VCO · switching base voice during play hands the same finger gesture to a different analog patch.
steps: In the EaganMatrix editor enable Dynamic Base Voice
Define subset A using W1/X1/Y1/Z1, subset B using W2/X2/Y2/Z2
Patch subset A into a lead voice (X1 -> VCO A V/oct, W1 -> env A)
Patch subset B into a bass voice (X2 -> VCO B V/oct, W2 -> env B)
Use a Continuum preset action (recirculator/macro) to switch base voice while playing
patchIdeas.signalOut: One active monophonic voice at a time, reassignable mid-performance between lead and bass patches.
patchIdeas.hear: A phrase that starts as a singing lead then cuts to a punchy bass tone on the same keys when you flip the base voice.
patchIdeas.diagram:
CVC:
* Dynamic Base Voice: ON
* Subset A: W1 X1 Y1 Z1
* Subset B: W2 X2 Y2 Z2

Lead VCO:
Lead Env:
Lead VCA:
Bass VCO:
Bass Env:
Bass VCA:

- Continuum (i2c) >> CVC (i2c IN)
- CVC (X1) p> Lead VCO (V/Oct)
- CVC (W1) g> Lead Env (Trig)
- CVC (Z1) >> Lead VCA (CV)
- CVC (X2) p> Bass VCO (V/Oct)
- CVC (W2) g> Bass Env (Trig)
- CVC (Z2) >> Bass VCA (CV)
